name: Genome Nexus
description: description='Genome Nexus is a comprehensive resource designed for the fast, automated, and high-throughput annotation and interpretation of genetic variants in cancer. It integrates information from various existing databases to convert DNA changes into protein changes, predict the functional effects of protein mutations, and provide insights into mutation frequencies, gene functions, variant effects, and clinical actionability. The project aims to facilitate researchers and clinicians in understanding genetic variations and their implications in cancer, thereby enhancing the efficiency of genomic analysis and interpretation.'
highLevelGoals: The stakeholder aims to streamline the process of analyzing genetic variants, enabling researchers to quickly understand the implications of these variants in cancer studies.
The stakeholder seeks to enhance the ability of clinicians to interpret genetic data, allowing them to make informed decisions regarding patient treatment based on genetic variations.

The stakeholder desires a system that consolidates data from multiple sources, ensuring that researchers and clinicians have access to the most relevant and up-to-date information regarding genetic variants.
The stakeholder insists on the capability to predict how genetic mutations affect protein function, which is crucial for understanding their impact on cancer and treatment options.
The stakeholder aims to automate the annotation process, reducing the time and effort required for genomic analysis and interpretation, thereby increasing productivity.
The stakeholder requires access to detailed information about how often certain mutations occur and their associated gene functions, which is vital for understanding cancer biology.
lowLevelGoals: Allow researchers to submit genetic variant data files to the system for processing and annotation.
Enable researchers to access detailed reports that include annotations and interpretations of uploaded genetic variants.
Allow clinicians to retrieve reports on genetic variants relevant to specific patients to inform treatment decisions.
Enable clinicians to upload patient-specific genetic data to receive insights and recommendations.
Allow the system to connect and pull data from various genetic databases to provide a unified view of genetic variants.
Allow researchers to search for specific genetic variants and retrieve information from integrated databases.
Enable the system to analyze genetic mutations and predict their impact on protein function for clinical decision-making.
Enable researchers to set parameters for automated annotation processes to enhance efficiency in genomic analysis.
Allow researchers to retrieve statistics on how often specific mutations occur in cancer studies.
Enable researchers to access information about gene functions associated with specific mutations in cancer research.
